data.narrative:
Pyka is a Series B autonomous drone company founded by Michael Norcia, Chuma Ogunwole, Kyle Moore, and Nathan White. The target operates within the Drones & Autonomous sector, which comprises 7 identified private peers (including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Manna Aero) and no direct public comparables. The company has raised $40M to date.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method due to peer-set constraints. The Stage-Progression approach uses Pyka's $40M total raised as a proxy base value, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B progression to the next funding stage, yielding an implied valuation of $76M. A formal Peer-Median method could not be applied: while four same-sector peers exist, none met the filtering threshold (requiring three or more peers at identical stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) needed to produce a reliable median. Broadening to all-sector medians would introduce excessive noise given the heterogeneity of the Drones & Autonomous universe.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $65M (low) to $87M (high), with a median point of $76M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method and underscores the primary valuation uncertainty: the absence of a sufficiently sized comparable-company cohort at Series B in the autonomous drone space. Future rounds will benefit materially from expanded peer data or business-specific metrics (revenue, unit economics, addressable-market penetration).
